Short description: Inject AI forecasts into preregistration or design workflows and observe which design decisions actually change.
Empirical unit of analysis: Preregistrations, forecast logs, design revisions, ex post results
Method traditions: Experimental economics; political science; policy evaluation
Key typology question: Does predictive success alter what is worth identifying, or only improve calibration?
Observable artifacts / traces: Forecast logs, redesign memos, preregistration diffs, final outcomes
Key outcomes observed: Calibration, novelty, theoretical sharpness, changed outcome measures or estimands
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Forecast-informed redesign improves calibration without narrowing novelty or explanatory ambition
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Extra deliberation time may drive gains rather than the forecast itself
Why this case has high leverage: This is the most direct case for whether predictive AI changes design judgment rather than merely speeds it.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
